spec_match.rationale: The page claims that JS only updates custom properties and CSS handles the math, but in reality, no CSS progress() formulas are declared in the stylesheet. Instead, Javascript manually does the math and writes inline style widths and colors, and the code boxes display obsolete comma-separated syntax.

interactive.rationale: Interactive with four sliders, but the interactivity is entirely powered by standard JavaScript math rather than CSS progress().

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb claims it showcases native composition, but the implementation is completely JS-driven.

design_system.rationale: Conforms to CSS variables design system.

openQuestions.title: Refactor Range Slider to use true CSS-driven progress() equations
openQuestions.detail: The page claims that the JavaScript only updates `--value` and all visual consequences flow from CSS. In reality, the stylesheet has no `progress()` formulas, and JS event listeners manually calculate width and color and write inline styles. Please refactor the styles to use true native `progress(var(--value) from var(--min) to var(--max))` expressions, and have the JS only update the custom property value, with the current manual JS math acting only as a graceful fallback if `CSS.supports()` returns false.

openQuestions.title: Fix incorrect comma syntax in progress() code examples
openQuestions.detail: The static code boxes display incorrect comma-separated syntax: `progress(var(--value), 0, 100)`. Update these examples to use standard `from ... to ...` keyword syntax: `progress(var(--value) from 0 to 100)`.

summary: A highly interactive range slider suite featuring four diverse visual indicators. However, the implementation is a major conformance failure: the claims of CSS-driven calculations are false since it relies entirely on manual JS style updates, and the code boxes display invalid comma-separated progress() syntax.
